1.0.title: INTEGRATE CAREER AND TECHNICAL STUDENT ORGANIZATIONS (CTSOs)

1.1.title: Explore the History and Organization of CTSOs

1.1.1.description: Discuss the requirements of CTSO participation/involvement as described in Carl D. Perkins Law (Level 1 (L1), Level 2 (L2), Complementary )

1.1.3.description: Investigate the impact of federal and state government regarding the progression and operation of CTSOs (e.g., Federal Statutes and Regulations, Nevada Administrative Code [NAC], Nevada Revised Statutes [NRS])

1.2.1.description: Discuss the purpose of parliamentary procedure

1.2.2.description: Demonstrate the proper use of parliamentary procedure

1.2.3.description: Differentiate between an office and a committee

1.2.4.description: Discuss the importance of participation in local, regional, state, and national conferences, events, and competitions

1.2.5.description: Participate in local, regional, state, or national conferences, events, or competitions

1.2.6.description: Describe the importance of a constitution and bylaws to the operation of a CTSO chapter

1.3.1.description: Explore opportunities in community service-related work-based learning (WBL)

1.3.2.description: Participate in a service learning (program related) and/or community service project or activity

1.3.3.description: Engage with business and industry partners for community service

1.4.1.description: Demonstrate college and career readiness (e.g., applications, resumes, interview skills, presentation skills)

1.4.2.description: Describe the appropriate professional/workplace attire and its importance

1.4.3.description: Investigate industry-standard credentials/certifications available within this Career Cluster™

1.4.4.description: Participate in authentic contextualized instructional activities

1.4.5.description: Demonstrate technical skills in various student organization activities/events

1.5.title: Understand the Relevance of Career and Technical Education (CTE)

1.5.1.description: Make a connection between program standards to career pathway(s)

1.5.2.description: Explain the importance of participation and completion of a program of study

1.5.3.description: Promote community awareness of local student organizations associated with CTE programs

2.1.title: Understand How Advances in Technology Impact Game

2.1.1.description: Explain the history of computing technologies that impact the game development industry

2.1.3.description: Research the evolution of video games

2.1.5.description: Evaluate contributions of individual game designers and developers

2.2.title: Understand Careers in Game Design and Development

2.2.1.description: Explore careers in designing and developing interactive experiences

2.2.2.description: Research careers in non-interactive media using real-time game engines, including Architectural, Engineering and Construction (AEC), virtual production, architectural visualization, simulation, product/industrial design, and advertising

2.2.3.description: Describe career pathways in software engineering, quality assurance, and testing

2.2.4.description: Review roles of the producer, designer, art director, tech artist, and programmer

2.2.5.description: Explain the career path of an independent developer

2.2.6.description: Research labor market information in related industries

3.0.title: FOUNDATIONS OF GAME DESIGN AND DEVELOPMENT

3.1.1.description: Explain the interdependence of team members between artistic, technical, and production disciplines

3.1.2.description: Outline the process of developing a game from concept to delivery and support

3.1.3.description: Compare various types of collaboration tools, e.g., version control, shared storage, cloud services

3.1.4.description: Explain the value of version control

3.1.5.description: Explain the purpose of vertical slice

3.2.1.description: Explore the components of game structure

3.2.2.description: Analyze the essentials of storytelling, including visual and environmental story telling

3.2.3.description: Explain the characteristics of a nonlinear story

3.2.4.description: Create rules for a game, e.g., levels and/or interactive flow

3.2.6.description: Develop objectives and outcomes for a game

3.2.7.description: Explain the importance of usability and how it impacts user experience

3.2.8.description: Explain in-game economies, motivators, and point systems

3.3.1.description: Research various styles of game documentation

3.3.3.description: Develop components of a game design document (GDD)

3.3.4.description: Develop a list of required game assets

3.4.2.description: Research victory condition mechanics of a game

3.4.3.description: Discuss relationships between game mechanics, game play, and interactivity

3.4.4.description: Investigate what makes a game engaging, “fun,” and playable to the user

4.1.1.description: Evaluate the use of layout and composition

4.1.5.description: Compare design considerations for environmental, assets, characters, and User Interface (UI) creation

4.1.6.description: Describe the characteristics and purposes of 2D, 2.5D, and 3D art

4.1.7.description: Evaluate the importance of artistic style and implement it with continuity

4.2.1.description: Identify components of a level and its environment

4.2.2.description: Compare processes of creating interior versus exterior environments

4.2.3.description: Compare level design of linear games to open world games

4.2.4.description: Research terrains for a specific environment

4.2.5.description: Discuss the concept of leveling up with increasing complexity

4.2.6.description: Describe the impact of story (explicit, implicit, and emergent) on level design

4.2.7.description: Explain the importance of flow, distance, timing, and choke points

4.2.8.description: Storyboard levels including flow and choke points

4.3.1.description: Investigate the twelve major character archetypes

4.3.2.description: Contrast architypes versus stereotypes as they relate to diversity and inclusion

4.3.3.description: Describe basic character backstory, strengths, and weaknesses

4.3.4.description: Explain the visual design of characters in relation to the “game feel” and artistic style

4.3.5.description: Describe the connection between character arc and character progression

4.3.6.description: Conceptualize and illustrate original game characters and assets

4.3.7.description: Examine the roles, purpose, and design of non-player characters (NPC)

4.3.8.description: Explain the difference in design between static and dynamic game objects, e.g., props, decorations versus characters, morphing objects

4.3.9.description: Detail the difference between organic and hard surfaces

4.4.2.description: Assemble immersive elements into a game

4.4.3.description: Establish a reward system and in-game economics

4.4.4.description: Apply game mechanics to a game world

5.1.1.description: Explain basic logic statements (e.g., if/then, cause/effect)

5.1.2.description: Explain the purpose and use of functions

5.1.4.description: Describe uses of Boolean operators and symbols associated with them

5.1.5.description: Demonstrate proper use of order of operations

5.1.6.description: Use logical thinking to create a diagram of code execution

5.1.7.description: Describe various types of loop structures used in programming

5.1.8.description: Describe the differences between compiled and interpreted code

5.1.9.description: Explain methods for producing artificial intelligence (AI) to control Non-Playable Characters (NPC)

5.1.10.description: Research design patterns in game programming (single, factory, and state)

5.2.2.description: Identify differences between compile and runtime errors

5.2.4.description: Describe how arrays are used to store objects in a list

5.2.6.description: Identify expected input and output of methods/functions

5.2.7.description: Explain the connection between visual programming and coding/scripting

5.2.8.description: Compare the use of constants and variables

5.2.9.description: Describe the implications of access modifiers (private/public, local/global)

5.2.10.description: Explore engine/programming documentation to understand available methods/functions

5.3.1.description: Utilize code to modify objects based on collision detection and player activation

5.3.2.description: Develop code or visual script that changes aspects of player movement (e.g., sprint, jump)

5.3.3.description: Develop code or visual script that responds to a graphic user interface (GUI) input (e.g., user interface design (UI) button press

5.3.4.description: Develop code or visual script that responds to hardware input (e.g., keyboard key or mouse press)

5.3.5.description: Generate test cases and expected results

5.3.6.description: Format and display the value of a variable to a GUI

5.3.7.description: Implement a basic point system for a game using visual scripting or code

5.3.8.description: Verify game functionality through testing and debugging

6.1.title: Explore Various Development Environment and Best Practices Per

6.1.1.description: Explain the characteristics and major components of game engines (render, compiler, editor)

6.1.2.description: Research various game engines for a software platform, target hardware, game style, or genre

6.1.3.description: Research supplemental tools necessary to create a specific development pipeline

6.1.4.description: Describe a complete game pipeline including the use of primary and supplemental tools and how they are used

6.1.5.description: Compare world scales between software tools

6.1.6.description: Describe considerations for importing and exporting assets

6.1.7.description: Define a standard directory structure and file naming conventions

6.2.1.description: Define the type, structure, and size of player environment

6.2.2.description: Place and define non-player characters (NPC) into the environment

6.2.3.description: Build boundaries, borders, and obstacles of levels within the game

6.2.4.description: Place triggers and develop scripted events

6.2.5.description: Create multiple levels for a game including start and closing screens and playable level(s)

6.3.1.description: Research examples of GUI in digital and real-world environments (e.g., Heads Up Display [HUD] and road signs)

6.3.2.description: Define and implement required elements for various GUIs

6.3.3.description: Create flowcharts that map the GUIs’ functionality

6.3.4.description: Design GUIs that use standard text, 2-D, and 3-D elements (buttons, progress bars, icons, etc.)

6.4.2.description: Change an object’s state or position over time

6.4.3.description: Establish an object’s relative speed (timing versus frame rate)

6.4.4.description: Describe the difference between global time and local time

6.4.5.description: Describe the difference between forward and inverse kinematics

6.4.6.description: Examine the process of particle creation and its application to game design

6.4.7.description: Explain how joints, sockets, bones, and skins are used

6.4.9.description: Simulate rigid body dynamics (e.g., shattering wall, breaking glass)

6.4.11.description: Describe the process of motion capture for animation

6.4.12.description: Practice the use of cinematics and camera movements in game engines

6.5.1.description: Integrate different types of audio (e.g., sound effects, ambient background, dialogue, user experience design (UX), and score)

6.5.2.description: Practice creating triggers for sound, mixes, and/or loops

6.5.3.description: Determine acceptable media formats and files for game development (e.g., sound, graphics, video)

6.5.4.description: Identify and import appropriate media into a game engine

6.6.1.description: Explain the application of low polygon and high polygon construction for real-time environments

6.6.2.description: Create game assets from references and game design documents (GDD)

6.6.3.description: Create hard surface and organic models using design principles (props or characters)

6.6.4.description: Apply texturing/surfacing/shading/grudging to models and normal mapping

6.6.5.description: Render or produce a high-resolution screenshot from within a game engine

6.6.6.description: Real-time render a video, animated sequence or cutscene in a game engine

6.6.7.description: Differentiate UVW mapping and world coordinate systems

6.6.8.description: Explain the importance of pre-baking lights and shadows for real-time interactive environments

6.6.9.description: Implement basic lighting for ambient and artificial light

6.6.10.description: Create an environment including terrain, foliage and/or static objects

6.6.12.description: Describe the use of Level of Detail (LOD) substitution, MIP mapping textures, and culling objects

7.0.title: LEGAL AND ETHICAL ISSUES IN GAME DESIGN AND DEVELOPMENT

7.1.title: Understand Legal Considerations in Game Development

7.1.1.description: Research laws that govern intellectual property in diverse forms

7.1.2.description: Evaluate Creative Commons and open-source licensure

7.1.3.description: Explain copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property protection

7.1.4.description: Identify key elements of non-disclosure agreements (NDA) and contracts

7.1.5.description: Adhere to intellectual property laws and regulations and cite proprietary content and derivative works

7.1.6.description: Explain Entertainment Software Rating Board (ESRB) and other rating systems for digital games

7.2.title: Explain Security Issues in Relation to Game Development and

7.2.1.description: Explain invasion of privacy in the use of technology

7.2.2.description: Explore the issues of piracy and digital rights management (DRM)

7.3.title: Understand the Importance of Ethics, Diversity, and Inclusion

7.3.1.description: Discuss diversity and inclusivity in games and the gaming industry

7.3.2.description: Discuss social responsibility and issues concerning video gaming

8.0.title: ADVANCED TOPICS IN DIGITAL GAME DEVELOPMENT

8.1.1.description: Describe integration of social components in a game

8.1.2.description: Explain the role of social media in the gaming community

8.1.3.description: Describe professional events in digital gaming

8.2.2.description: Evaluate the advances of multi-player gaming

8.3.2.description: Examine current trends in output devices and displays
